query:
Research the company "stanleyblackanddecker".

Research the company's recent financial and operational performance: (1) What was their most recent quarterly or annual financial result, and was there a significant miss vs. guidance or expectations? (2) What is their current revenue/scale (Fortune 500, $10B+, etc.)? (3) Have they announced a new CEO or major C-suite leadership change in the last 6-12 months? (4) Has the company recently pulled, reduced, or delayed 2026 guidance or any forward projections? (5) What major product launches or platform expansions are they executing in their current fiscal year? (6) What is their production growth rate or capacity expansion status? (7) Have they announced cost-cutting, efficiency, or restructuring initiatives? (8) What are their key business segments, and are there known misalignments between individual function performance (marketing ROAS, sales lift, pricing realization) and overall P&L results? (9) What distribution or go-to-market changes have they announced? Focus on earnings transcripts, investor relations materials, and recent news.

Focus on the last 6 months. Return a comprehensive summary with specific facts, numbers, and dates.
answer:
**Executive Summary**
Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) has recently undergone significant strategic and leadership transitions while delivering a strong Q1 2026 financial performance that largely beat consensus estimates. The company is actively executing a major supply chain restructuring to reduce its reliance on China, divesting non-core assets like its Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing (CAM) business, and transitioning its gas-powered outdoor products to a licensing model. Christopher Nelson took over as CEO in late 2025, steering the company toward a target of 35% gross margins by late 2026 through aggressive cost-cutting, SKU rationalization, and footprint reductions.

**Financial Performance and Scale**
*   **Revenue and Scale:** Stanley Black & Decker is currently ranked 294th on the 2026 Fortune 500 list with $15.1 billion in reported revenue [15 CT companies make the 2026 Fortune 500](https://hartfordbusiness.com/article/15-ct-companies-make-the-2026-fortune-500/). The company's ranking has steadily declined from 263rd ($15.8 billion) in 2024 and 283rd ($15.4 billion) in 2025 [These 15 Connecticut-based companies made the 2025 Fortune 500 list of largest U.S. corporations](https://www.ctinsider.com/business/article/ct-companies-fortune-500-cigna-interactive-brokers-20353198.php). For the trailing twelve months ending March 31, 2026, revenue was $15.232 billion, a slight 0.06% year-over-year decline [Stanley Black & Decker Revenue 2012-2026 | SWK | MacroTrends](https://www.macrotrends.net/stocks/charts/SWK/stanley-black-decker/revenue). 
*   **Q1 2026 Results:** The company reported Q1 2026 net sales of $3.85 billion (a 3% YoY increase, flat organically), beating consensus estimates of $3.75 billion to $3.78 billion [Stanley Black & Decker Reports Strong 1Q 2026 Results](https://ir.stanleyblackanddecker.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2026/Stanley-Black--Decker-Reports-Strong-1Q-2026-Results/default.aspx) [STANLEY BLACK & DECKER ($SWK) Releases Q1 2026 Earnings | Quiver Quantitative](https://www.quiverquant.com/news/STANLEY+BLACK+&+DECKER). Adjusted EPS was $0.80, significantly beating analyst estimates of $0.59 to $0.63 [Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) Tops Q1 EPS by 21c; Reaffirms 2026 Adjusted EPS Guidance](https://www.streetinsider.com/Earnings/Stanley+Black+&+Decker+(SWK)+Tops+Q1+EPS+by+21c;+Reaffirms+2026+Adjusted+EPS+Guidance/26386996.html). 
*   **Earnings Miss:** Despite top-line and EPS beats, Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA came in at $245.1 million, representing a 26.6% miss against analyst estimates of $334.1 million [Stanley Black & Decker’s (NYSE:SWK) Q1 CY2026 Sales Beat Estimates - StockStory](https://stockstory.org/us/stocks/nyse/swk/news/earnings/stanley-black-and-deckers-nyseswk-q1-cy2026-sales-beat-estimates).
*   **Segment Performance:** The Tools & Outdoor segment generated $3.34 billion in Q1 2026 revenue (a 2% increase) with an 8.7% adjusted margin [Stanley Black & Decker beats estimates on revenue growth](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/stanley-black--decker-beats-estimates-on-revenue-growth-93CH-4644166) [Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) lifts 2026 EPS outlook after $1.8B CAM sale](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/SWK/8-k-stanley-black-decker-inc-reports-material-event-c6df4f7f371f.html). The Engineered Fastening segment grew 10% to $511 million in revenue with a 12.0% adjusted margin [Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) lifts 2026 EPS outlook after $1.8B CAM sale](https://www.stocktitan.net/sec-filings/SWK/8-k-stanley-black-decker-inc-reports-material-event-c6df4f7f371f.html) [Stanley Black & Decker beats estimates on revenue growth](https://www.investing.com/news/earnings/stanley-black--decker-beats-estimates-on-revenue-growth-93CH-4644166).

**Guidance and Forward Projections**
*   **EPS Guidance Raised:** The company raised its full-year 2026 GAAP EPS guidance to $4.15–$5.35 (up from $3.15–$4.35) to account for gains from the CAM business divestiture [Stanley Black & Decker Reports Strong 1Q 2026 Results](https://ir.stanleyblackanddecker.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2026/Stanley-Black--Decker-Reports-Strong-1Q-2026-Results/default.aspx). Full-year 2026 adjusted EPS guidance was reaffirmed at $4.90 to $5.70 [Stanley Black & Decker Q1 2026 slides: earnings earnings beat, margins on track By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/stanley-black--decker-q1-2026-slides-earnings-beat-margins-on-track-93CH-4645501).
*   **Cash Flow Adjustments:** Free cash flow guidance for 2026 was adjusted to $500 million–$700 million to include projected taxes and fees from the CAM sale; excluding these, the core projection remains $700 million–$900 million [Stanley Black & Decker Reports Strong 1Q 2026 Results](https://ir.stanleyblackanddecker.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2026/Stanley-Black--Decker-Reports-Strong-1Q-2026-Results/default.aspx).
*   **Margin Targets:** Management is targeting a 35% gross margin by Q4 2026, relying on $350–$400 million in productivity gains and a 2% pricing benefit to offset approximately $800 million in annualized gross tariff costs [Stanley Black & Decker Inc (SWK) Q3 2025 Earnings Call Highlights](https://www.gurufocus.com/news/3182531/stanley-black-decker-inc-swk-q3-2025-earnings-call-highlights-navigating-challenges-with-strategic-growth-and-cost-efficiency) [Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) Earnings Transcript | The Motley Fool](https://www.fool.com/earnings/call-transcripts/2026/02/04/stanley-black-decker-swk-earnings-transcript/).

**Leadership Transitions**
*   **CEO Change:** Christopher Nelson, formerly COO and President of the Tools & Outdoor business, was appointed President and CEO effective October 1, 2025 [Stanley Black & Decker taps COO Christopher Nelson as next CEO | Reuters](https://www.reuters.com/sustainability/boards-policy-regulation/stanley-black-decker-taps-coo-christopher-nelson-next-ceo-2025-06-30/) [Stanley Black & Decker names new CEO | HBS Dealer](https://hbsdealer.com/stanley-black-decker-names-new-ceo-0). His 2026 compensation includes a $1.3 million base salary and $10.345 million in target equity awards [Connecticut-based toolmaker Stanley Black & Decker announces new CEO](https://www.ctinsider.com/business/article/stanley-black-decker-tools-new-ceo-chris-nelson-20400417.php).
*   **Board Restructuring:** Former CEO Donald Allan, Jr. transitioned to Executive Chair on October 1, 2025, and will retire on October 1, 2026 [Stanley Black & Decker Announces Board Leadership Changes and Appoints Shane M. O'Kelly as Director](https://www.prnewswire.com/news-releases/stanley-black--decker-announces-board-leadership-changes-and-appoints-shane-m-okelly-as-director-302669622.html) [Stanley Black & Decker - Stanley Black & Decker Announces Board Leadership Changes and Appoints Shane M. O'Kelly as Director](https://ir.stanleyblackanddecker.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2026/Stanley-Black--Decker-Announces-Board-Leadership-Changes-and-Appoints-Shane-M--OKelly-as-Director/default.aspx). Debra Crew was appointed Lead Independent Director in January 2026 and will succeed Allan as Chair of the Board in October 2026 [Stanley Black & Decker announces board leadership changes By Investing.com](https://www.investing.com/news/company-news/stanley-black--decker-announces-board-leadership-changes-93CH-4464765) [Stanley Black & Decker - Stanley Black & Decker Announces Board Leadership Changes and Appoints Shane M. O'Kelly as Director](https://ir.stanleyblackanddecker.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2026/Stanley-Black--Decker-Announces-Board-Leadership-Changes-and-Appoints-Shane-M--OKelly-as-Director/default.aspx). Additionally, Advance Auto Parts CEO Shane M. O'Kelly joined the Board in January 2026 [Stanley Black & Decker Announces Board Leadership Changes and Appoints Shane M. O'Kelly as Director - Jan 26, 2026](https://newsroom.stanleyblackanddecker.com/2026-01-26-Stanley-Black-Decker-Announces-Board-Leadership-Changes-and-Appoints-Shane-M-OKelly-as-Director).

**Operational Restructuring and Capacity Expansion**
*   **Supply Chain Shift:** SWK is aggressively reducing its U.S. supply chain exposure to China, aiming to drop from 15% in 2024 to less than 10% by mid-2026, and under 5% by the end of 2026 [Stanley Black & Decker leans on supply chain to hit financial goals](https://www.supplychaindive.com/news/stanley-black-decker-supply-chain-financial-goals/805198/) [SWK Q3 2025 Earnings Transcript - The Globe and Mail](https://www.theglobeandmail.com/investing/markets/stocks/SWK/pressreleases/1427963/swk-q3-2025-earnings-transcript/). This includes rapidly shifting cordless product manufacturing from China to Mexico [Stanley Black & Decker leans on supply chain to hit financial goals](https://www.supplychaindive.com/news/stanley-black-decker-supply-chain-financial-goals/805198/).
*   **Capacity Expansion:** The company is investing $4 million to expand its Mission, Texas manufacturing facility. The project, starting in October 2026 and finishing by year-end, will add a new assembly line, plastic injection molding, and 88 to 100 new full-time jobs [Stanley Black & Decker is investing $4M to expand its Mission plant and add 88 new jobs](https://www.rgvbusinessjournal.com/news/19/04/2026/stanley-black-decker-mission-expansion/) [Stanley Black & Decker to build $4 million expansion | Mission manufacturer will add new line; add 88 new jobs and retain 150](https://www.missionedc.com/news-and-media/latest-news/p/item/67189/stanley-black-&-decker-to-build-4-million-expansion-mission-manufacturer-will-add-new-line-add-88-new-jobs-and-retain-150) [Stanley Black & Decker hiring up to 100 workers as it expands Mission manufacturing plant](https://www.krgv.com/news/stanley-black-and-decker-hiring-up-to-100-workers-as-it-expands-mission-manufacturing-plant/).
*   **Cost-Cutting & Divestitures:** SWK successfully captured $2.1 billion in cumulative run-rate savings from a mid-2022 cost-reduction program [Stanley Black & Decker Sees Modest 2026 Growth After Margin-Driven Q4 Beat | AlphaStreet](https://news.alphastreet.com/stanley-black-decker-sees-modest-2026-growth-after-margin-driven-q4-beat/). The company is also implementing a plan to reduce its manufacturing footprint by 30% and cut its product SKUs by 40% [Stanley Black & Decker to slash SKUs, footprint in $1.5B savings plan](https://www.supplychaindive.com/news/stanley-black-decker-supply-chain-transformation/628494/). On April 6, 2026, SWK completed the sale of its CAM business to Howmet Aerospace for $1.8 billion in cash, utilizing $1.525 billion to $1.6 billion in net proceeds for debt reduction [Stanley Black & Decker Completes Sale of Consolidated Aerospace Manufacturing Business to Howmet Aerospace](https://ir.stanleyblackanddecker.com/news-events/press-releases/news-details/2026/Stanley-Black--Decker-Completes-Sale-of-Consolidated-Aerospace-Manufacturing-Business-to-Howmet-Aerospace/default.aspx) [Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) Earnings Transcript](https://finance.yahoo.com/news/stanley-black-decker-swk-earnings-143645147.html).

**Product Strategy and Go-To-Market Changes**
*   **Gas-Powered Phase-Out:** Starting in mid-2026, SWK is phasing out the manufacturing of its gas-powered walk-behind outdoor products, transitioning them to a licensing model. This go-to-market change is expected to reduce 2026 revenue by $120 million to $140 million, though management notes it will be margin accretive [Stanley Black & Decker Sees Modest 2026 Growth After Margin-Driven Q4 Beat | AlphaStreet](https://news.alphastreet.com/stanley-black-decker-sees-modest-2026-growth-after-margin-driven-q4-beat/) [Stanley Black & Decker beat expectations in 2025, but will reassess prices](https://www.manufacturingdive.com/news/stanley-black-decker-q4-2025-tariffs-prices-outlook-2026/811546/).
*   **New Launches:** The company launched the CRAFTSMAN V20* ADVANCED Batteries on May 21, 2026, targeting demanding DIY tasks [DEWALT owner Stanley Black & Decker CEO to speak at 2026 Wells Fargo event](https://www.stocktitan.net/news/SWK/stanley-black-decker-to-present-at-the-2026-wells-fargo-industrials-gjk1hg0xizbk.html). 
*   **Platform Expansion:** SWK is executing a European hand tools expansion for the Stanley brand in 2026 to capture market share and improve margins [Stanley Black & Decker (SWK) Earnings Transcript - AOL](https://www.aol.com/articles/stanley-black-decker-swk-earnings-143645985.html).

**Gaps and Limitations**
*   **Functional Misalignments:** The provided research does not contain specific data regarding misalignments between individual function performance (such as marketing ROAS or specific sales lift metrics) and overall P&L results. While pricing realization is mentioned as a 2% benefit to offset tariffs, no internal friction or misalignment is detailed in the findings.
*   **Production Growth Rate:** While capacity expansions (Mission, TX) and footprint reductions (30% cut) are detailed, an exact aggregate production growth rate percentage for the current fiscal year was not provided in the source material.
